title: S.O.F protocol for nuclei isolation from fresh and frozen tissues using OptiPrep® discontinuous gradient
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7043l","text":"This protocol is the result of the combination of various nuclei isolation protocols for single cell RNA-seq experiments using droplet-based methods, and is an extension of the Frankenstein (S.O.F means Son Of Frankenstein). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0ba7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a15j5","text":"Developed to prepare nuclei isolates from fresh and frozen material of small-to-large sizes. The good thing is that it does not uses FACS but OptiPrep® discontinuous gradient to remove debris. It is the alternative protocol when FACS is not available.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"77n6s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"96aa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3j5dt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

add 4 ml, 2.5 ml and 0.5 ml respectively of the Tricine, KCl and MgCl2•6H2O  stock solutions (above); adjust to pH 7.8 with 1 M KOH and make up to 100 ml.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dploh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aqev8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dhgci","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7394e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c57qp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erpld","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"98hh8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119307,"guid":"419820EB341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Nuclei EZ lysis buffer ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"EZ PREP NUC-101","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Mince/chop tissue with a razor blade to small pieces. The tissue may be as small as a grain of rice. For mincing the tissue, you may take the tube out of ice, however, be quick and return to ice.
Add 300 µL of chilled Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) to the tissue in 1.5 mL tube.
Gently homogenize the sample using a douncer by stroking 10-20 times. Keep tube on ice at all times.

Add an extra 900 µL of chilled Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL), mix gently by pipetting using wide-bore tips and incubate on ice for 00:05:00 . Repeat mixing 2-3 times during the incubation. RNAse inhibitor will now be in the range of 0.2-0.5 U/uL.

Filter homogenate using a 70 μm-strainer mesh to fit a 15 ml Falcon tube (e.g. pluriStrainer Mini 70 μm Cell Strainer). pluriStrainer Mini 70 μm (Cell Strainer, pluriSelect, 43-10070-40, https://www.pluriselect.com/us/pluristrainer-mini-70-m-cell-strainer.html#size=27 )
Transfer flow though into a 1.5 mL LoBind tube and centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C and remove supernatant leaving behind ~ 50 µL .
Optional: add 1 mL of EZ Lysis buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL), gently resuspend pellet and incubate for 00:05:00 on ice. Then centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Remove supernatant as much as possible without disturbing pellet (if pellet looks loose leave ~ 50 µL behind) and add 1 mL of Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) without resuspending .
Centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Optional: Remove supernatant as much as possible without disturbing pellet (if pellet looks loose leave ~ 50 µL behind) and add 500 µL of Nuclei Wash and Resuspension Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL) without resuspending . Centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .

Remove supernatant as much as possible without disturbing pellet (if pellet looks loose leave ~ 50 µL behind) and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of G5 buffer and transfer onto a 10 mL LoBind centrifuge tube. Then add another mL of G5 (final volume 2 mL).

Carefully, underlayer the sample with 2 mL of the 30% iodixanol and then 2 mL of the 35% iodixanol, as shown in the picture below. Layering the gradient. Image adapted from OptiPrep Application Sheet S10 .

Centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:20:00 at 4 °C using swinging bucket centrifuge with break set to OFF. (Note: using is also possible). Position on nuclei pellet. Image adapted from OptiPrep Application Sheet S10 .

After centrifugation, a white-ish band of nuclei between the 30%-35% iodixanol interface should be visible. Aspirate the top layers down until the white nuclei band at the interphase of 29%-35%. Using 200 uL tip, collect the nuclei band and transfer to a fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube.
Then top up to ~1.3 mL with Wash and Resuspension Buffer (supplemented with RNAse Inhibitor 0.2-0.5 U/uL), mix well but gently, and centrifuge the nuclei at for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Remove supernatant and repeat once more for a total of 2 washes.

Check integrity and purity under microscope and count manually or using an automatic counter. For automatic counter I recommend LUNA-FL™ Dual Fluorescence Cell Counter and Acridine Orange/Propidium Iodide (AO/PI) Cell Viability Kit (F23001).
Proceed to your amazing snRNA-Seq experiment!
creator: Luciano G Martelotto
